actions.name: filter
actions.label: Filter
actions.description: Filter events based on their content

configuration.name: expression
configuration.description: Boolean expression to filter data
configuration.label: Filter Expression

configuration.name: expression
configuration.description: Boolean expression to evaluate
configuration.label: Condition

actions.name: loop
actions.label: Loop
actions.description: Repeat downstream steps until a condition is met

configuration.name: untilExpression
configuration.description: Boolean expression that must evaluate to true to stop looping
configuration.label: Until Expression

configuration.name: maxIterations
configuration.description: Maximum number of iterations before the loop stops
configuration.label: Max Iterations

configuration.name: timeoutSeconds
configuration.description: Maximum total wall-clock time for a single loop run. The run fails if it is still looping after this many seconds.
configuration.label: Timeout (seconds)

configuration.name: delayBetweenIterations
configuration.description: Wait before starting the next iteration
configuration.label: Delay between iterations

schema.name: enabled
schema.description: Wait between loop iterations before emitting on Next again.
schema.label: Enable delay

schema.name: intervalSeconds
schema.description: Seconds to wait before the next iteration
schema.label: Delay interval (seconds)

actions.name: runnerBash
actions.label: Run Bash
actions.description: Runs a Bash script on a fleet runner with upstream node data in SUPERPLANE_PAYLOAD_FILE

configuration.name: execution_mode
configuration.description: Where the script runs: on the runner machine, or inside a container.
configuration.label: Execution mode

options.label: Host
options.description: Runs with Bash on the runner. The fleet image must include Bash.

options.label: Docker
options.description: Runs in an isolated container from the image below.

configuration.name: docker_image_preset
configuration.description: Pick a base image with Bash, or choose Other to type your own registry reference.
configuration.label: Container base image

configuration.name: docker_image
configuration.description: Full OCI image reference when you chose Other above.
configuration.label: Custom container image

configuration.name: enable_setup_commands
configuration.description: Run shell commands before the Bash script.
configuration.label: Run setup commands

configuration.name: setup_commands
configuration.description: One shell command per line. Runs before the script with the same environment variables.
configuration.label: Setup commands

configuration.name: script
configuration.description: Bash executed by the runner. Write JSON to SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE; read upstream data from SUPERPLANE_PAYLOAD_FILE.
configuration.defaultValue:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "Hello world"
echo '{"example":"output"}' > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
configuration.label: Script

configuration.name: environment
configuration.description: Optional key/value pairs passed into the script environment
configuration.label: Environment variables

schema.name: valueSource
schema.description: Where this variable value comes from
schema.label: Value source

schema.name: value
schema.description: Literal value. Supports expressions such as {{ previous().data.author.email }}
schema.label: Value

schema.name: secret
schema.description: Stored credential key to use as the variable value
schema.label: Secret key

configuration.name: execution_timeout_seconds
configuration.description: Hard time limit for the whole task, including image pull and script run. Defaults to 3600 seconds (1 hour).
configuration.label: Execution timeout (seconds)

widgets.name: annotation
widgets.label: Annotation
widgets.description: Add text annotations and notes to your workflow for documentation and clarity

configuration.name: text
configuration.description: Text content for the annotation
configuration.defaultValue:
## Annotation

How to use annotations:

- Annotations are displayed as sticky notes in the canvas editor
- It supports basic markdown formatting
configuration.label: Annotation Text

configuration.name: channel
configuration.description: Discord channel to send the message to
configuration.label: Channel

configuration.name: content
configuration.description: Plain text message content (max 2000 characters)
configuration.label: Content

configuration.name: embedTitle
configuration.description: Title for the rich embed

configuration.name: embedDescription

configuration.name: embedColor

configuration.label: Embed Color

configuration.name: embedUrl
configuration.description: URL to link from the embed title
configuration.label: Embed URL

configuration.name: files
configuration.description: Files to attach: download from a URL, or attach inline content such as an AI component artifact
configuration.label: Files

schema.name: url
schema.description: Public http(s) URL; the file is downloaded and attached
schema.label: URL

schema.name: encoding
schema.description: Match the artifact's encoding field: text for plain text, base64 for binary files
schema.label: Encoding

schema.name: mimeType
schema.description: Used to name the attachment when no filename is set
schema.label: MIME Type

schema.name: filename
schema.description: Optional attachment name; defaults to a name derived from the URL or MIME type
schema.label: Filename

configuration.name: botToken
configuration.description: Discord bot token from the Developer Portal
configuration.label: Bot Token

integrations.instructions:
To set up Discord integration:

1. Go to the **Discord Developer Portal** (https://discord.com/developers/applications)
2. Click **New Application** and give it a name
3. Go to **OAuth2** → **URL Generator**:
   - Under **Scopes**, select **bot**
   - Under **Bot Permissions**, select **View Channels**, **Send Messages**, **Attach Files**, and **Read Message History**. **Attach Files** is required for the Send Text Message component to upload file attachments.
   - Copy the generated URL and open it in a new tab to invite the bot to your server
4. Go to the **Bot** section:
   - Click **Add Bot** (if not already added)
   - Uncheck the **Public Bot** option
   - Under **Token**, click **Reset Token** then **Copy** to get your bot token
5. Paste the **Bot Token** in the **Bot Token** field below

configuration.text:
## Main Entrypoint

Two ways to trigger:

1/ Mention @superplanehq
2/ Add "factory" label

## Babysit

Starting the CI process. The agents attempts 5 times to fix any issue that was pointed out by CI.

configuration.head: {{ $["Create Branch"].data.result.branch }}
configuration.title: fix: {{ root().data.issue.title }}

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${GITHUB_TOKEN:?GITHUB_TOKEN is required}"
: "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:?ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is required}"
: "${REPO:?REPO is required}"
: "${BRANCH:?BRANCH is required}"
ISSUE_NUMBER="${ISSUE_NUMBER:-unknown}"
ISSUE_TITLE="${ISSUE_TITLE:-}"
RAW="$(mktemp)"

command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "git not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }

# Claude is not installed on the host: install the Claude Code CLI (user-level, no root needed).
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.claude/bin:$PATH"
command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "Claude Code install failed" >&2; exit 1; }

git config --global user.email "automation@superplane.dev"
git config --global user.name "SuperPlane Automation"

WORKDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
git clone --depth 1 --branch "$BRANCH" \
  "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${REPO}.git" "$WORKDIR"
cd "$WORKDIR"

# DCO: force a Signed-off-by trailer on EVERY commit in this repo, including Claude Code's own internal commits.
mkdir -p .git/hooks
cat > .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg <<'HOOK'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
MSG_FILE="$1"
SOB="Signed-off-by: SuperPlane Automation <automation@superplane.dev>"
grep -qF "$SOB" "$MSG_FILE" || printf '\n%s\n' "$SOB" >> "$MSG_FILE"
HOOK
chmod +x .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg

read -r -d '' PROMPT <<EOF || true
You are implementing a fix for a GitHub issue on the current branch.

Repository: ${REPO}
Issue number: ${ISSUE_NUMBER}
Issue title: ${ISSUE_TITLE}

1/ Gather context. Using the provided GitHub token, read the FULL issue and ALL of its comments before writing any code.
2/ Implement. Make the code changes required to resolve the issue and address the discussion in the comments.
3/ Keep the change focused and commit your work to the current branch with clear commit messages.

Guidelines:

1/ Focus on clean code.
2/ Make long term fixes, not fast patches
3/ Add tests where it makes sense.

The output should contain a bullet point summary of changes in the following format:

{
   "summary": [
      "example bullet point 1",
      "example bullet point 2",
   }
}

Output only the JSON result. Keep the bulletpoints short, concise and to the point.
EOF

# Human-readable formatter for Claude Code stream-json events.
cat > /tmp/sp_format.py <<'PYEOF'
import sys, json
for line in sys.stdin:
    line = line.strip()
    if not line:
        continue
    try:
        e = json.loads(line)
    except Exception:
        print(line, flush=True)
        continue
    t = e.get("type")
    if t == "system":
        print("=== session started (model=%s) ===" % e.get("model", "?"), flush=True)
    elif t == "assistant":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            bt = b.get("type")
            if bt == "text":
                txt = (b.get("text") or "").strip()
                if txt:
                    print("\n" + txt, flush=True)
            elif bt == "tool_use":
                inp = b.get("input", {}) or {}
                detail = inp.get("command") or inp.get("file_path") or inp.get("path") or json.dumps(inp)[:200]
                print("  -> %s: %s" % (b.get("name"), detail), flush=True)
    elif t == "user":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            if b.get("type") == "tool_result":
                c = b.get("content", "")
                if isinstance(c, list):
                    c = " ".join(x.get("text", "") for x in c if isinstance(x, dict))
                c = str(c).strip().replace("\n", " ")
                if c:
                    print("     <- %s" % c[:300], flush=True)
    elif t == "result":
        ok = "ok" if not e.get("is_error") else "ERROR"
        print("\n=== done [%s] turns=%s cost=$%s ===" % (ok, e.get("num_turns"), e.get("total_cost_usd")), flush=True)
PYEOF

# Extractor for the final result JSON from the stream.
cat > /tmp/sp_extract.py <<'PYEOF'
import json, os
summary = ""
try:
    with open(os.environ["RAW"]) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                e = json.loads(line)
            except Exception:
                continue
            if e.get("type") == "result":
                summary = e.get("result", "")
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass
print(json.dumps({"branch": os.environ.get("BRANCH", ""), "summary": summary}))
PYEOF

# Run Claude Code with live, human-readable streaming; keep raw JSONL for result extraction.
if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose --output-format stream-json 2>&1 \
    | tee "$RAW" \
    | python3 -u /tmp/sp_format.py || true
else
  echo "python3 not found; streaming raw JSON" >&2
  claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose --output-format stream-json 2>&1 \
    | tee "$RAW" || true
fi

git add -A
git commit -s -m "fix: implement issue #${ISSUE_NUMBER} via Claude Code" || echo "Nothing new to commit"
git push origin "$BRANCH"
echo "Pushed implementation to ${BRANCH}"

# Emit node output: branch plus the final summary from Claude.
if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  RAW="$RAW" python3 /tmp/sp_extract.py > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
else
  printf '{"branch":"%s"}\n' "$BRANCH" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
fi

configuration.body:
Taking a look!
Follow my progress: {{ $["Open Draft PR"].data.html_url }}

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${GITHUB_TOKEN:?GITHUB_TOKEN is required}"
: "${REPO:?REPO is required}"
: "${PR_NUMBER:?PR_NUMBER is required}"
: "${PROJECT_OWNER:?PROJECT_OWNER is required}"
: "${PROJECT_NUMBER:?PROJECT_NUMBER is required}"
STATUS_FIELD="${STATUS_FIELD:-Status}"
TARGET_STATUS="${TARGET_STATUS:-Merged}"
REQUIRE_LABEL="${REQUIRE_LABEL:-}"
ADD_IF_MISSING="${ADD_IF_MISSING:-false}"

command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "curl not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }
command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "python3 not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }

OWNER="${REPO%%/*}"
NAME="${REPO##*/}"

gql() {
  local resp
  resp="$(curl -sS -X POST "https://api.github.com/graphql" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "$(QUERY="$1" VARS="$2" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"query":os.environ["QUERY"],"variables":json.loads(os.environ["VARS"])}))')")"
  RESP="$resp" python3 -c 'import os,sys,json
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
if d.get("errors"):
    sys.stderr.write("GraphQL error: %s\n" % json.dumps(d["errors"]))
    sys.exit(1)' || { echo "$resp" >&2; exit 1; }
  printf '%s' "$resp"
}

# 1) Resolve project id + Status single-select field id + target option id.
PROJ_Q='query($owner:String!,$number:Int!,$field:String!){organization(login:$owner){projectV2(number:$number){id field(name:$field){... on ProjectV2SingleSelectField{id options{id name}}}}}}'
PROJ_V="$(POWNER="$PROJECT_OWNER" PNUM="$PROJECT_NUMBER" PFIELD="$STATUS_FIELD" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"owner":os.environ["POWNER"],"number":int(os.environ["PNUM"]),"field":os.environ["PFIELD"]}))')"
PROJ_RESP="$(gql "$PROJ_Q" "$PROJ_V")"
eval "$(RESP="$PROJ_RESP" TARGET="$TARGET_STATUS" python3 -c 'import os,json,sys
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
p=((d.get("data") or {}).get("organization") or {}).get("projectV2")
if not p:
    sys.stderr.write("Project not found for owner/number\n"); sys.exit(1)
f=p.get("field") or {}
if not f.get("id"):
    sys.stderr.write("Status field not found (check STATUS_FIELD name)\n"); sys.exit(1)
opt=""
for o in f.get("options",[]):
    if o["name"].strip().lower()==os.environ["TARGET"].strip().lower():
        opt=o["id"]; break
if not opt:
    sys.stderr.write("Target status option not found\n"); sys.exit(1)
print("PROJECT_ID=%s" % json.dumps(p["id"]))
print("FIELD_ID=%s" % json.dumps(f["id"]))
print("OPTION_ID=%s" % json.dumps(opt))')"

# 2) Resolve the PR node id, labels, and existing project item.
PR_Q='query($owner:String!,$name:String!,$pr:Int!){repository(owner:$owner,name:$name){pullRequest(number:$pr){id labels(first:50){nodes{name}} projectItems(first:50){nodes{id project{id number}}}}}}'
PR_V="$(POWNER="$OWNER" PNAME="$NAME" PPR="$PR_NUMBER" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"owner":os.environ["POWNER"],"name":os.environ["PNAME"],"pr":int(os.environ["PPR"])}))')"
PR_RESP="$(gql "$PR_Q" "$PR_V")"
eval "$(RESP="$PR_RESP" PID="$PROJECT_ID" REQ="$REQUIRE_LABEL" python3 -c 'import os,json,sys
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
pr=((d.get("data") or {}).get("repository") or {}).get("pullRequest")
if not pr:
    sys.stderr.write("PR not found\n"); sys.exit(1)
req=os.environ.get("REQ","").strip().lower()
labels=[(n.get("name") or "").strip().lower() for n in (pr.get("labels") or {}).get("nodes",[])]
has="1" if (not req or req in labels) else "0"
item=""
for n in (pr.get("projectItems") or {}).get("nodes",[]):
    if ((n.get("project") or {}).get("id"))==os.environ["PID"]:
        item=n["id"]; break
print("PR_NODE_ID=%s" % json.dumps(pr["id"]))
print("ITEM_ID=%s" % json.dumps(item))
print("HAS_LABEL=%s" % has)')"

# Gate on the required label.
if [ "$HAS_LABEL" != "1" ]; then
  echo "PR #${PR_NUMBER} is missing required label '${REQUIRE_LABEL}'; skipping."
  printf '{"moved":false,"reason":"missing_label","pr_number":"%s"}\n' "$PR_NUMBER" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
  exit 0
fi

# 3) If the PR is not on the board, optionally add it (default: skip).
if [ -z "$ITEM_ID" ]; then
  if [ "$ADD_IF_MISSING" = "true" ]; then
    echo "PR not on board yet; adding it to project ${PROJECT_NUMBER}"
    ADD_Q='mutation($project:ID!,$content:ID!){addProjectV2ItemById(input:{projectId:$project,contentId:$content}){item{id}}}'
    ADD_V="$(PPROJ="$PROJECT_ID" PCONTENT="$PR_NODE_ID" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"project":os.environ["PPROJ"],"content":os.environ["PCONTENT"]}))')"
    ADD_RESP="$(gql "$ADD_Q" "$ADD_V")"
    ITEM_ID="$(RESP="$ADD_RESP" python3 -c 'import os,json;d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"]);print((((d.get("data") or {}).get("addProjectV2ItemById") or {}).get("item") or {}).get("id",""))')"
    if [ -z "$ITEM_ID" ]; then echo "Failed to add PR to project" >&2; exit 1; fi
  else
    echo "PR #${PR_NUMBER} is not on project ${PROJECT_NUMBER}; skipping."
    printf '{"moved":false,"reason":"not_on_board","pr_number":"%s"}\n' "$PR_NUMBER" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
    exit 0
  fi
fi

# 4) Move the card: set the Status single-select field to the target option.
UPD_Q='mutation($project:ID!,$item:ID!,$field:ID!,$opt:String!){updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue(input:{projectId:$project,itemId:$item,fieldId:$field,value:{singleSelectOptionId:$opt}}){projectV2Item{id}}}'
UPD_V="$(PPROJ="$PROJECT_ID" PITEM="$ITEM_ID" PFIELD="$FIELD_ID" POPT="$OPTION_ID" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"project":os.environ["PPROJ"],"item":os.environ["PITEM"],"field":os.environ["PFIELD"],"opt":os.environ["POPT"]}))')"
gql "$UPD_Q" "$UPD_V" >/dev/null

echo "Moved PR #${PR_NUMBER} to '${TARGET_STATUS}'."
printf '{"moved":true,"pr_number":"%s","status":"%s"}\n' "$PR_NUMBER" "$TARGET_STATUS" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${GITHUB_TOKEN:?GITHUB_TOKEN is required}"
: "${REPO:?REPO is required}"
: "${PR_NUMBER:?PR_NUMBER is required}"
: "${PROJECT_OWNER:?PROJECT_OWNER is required}"
: "${PROJECT_NUMBER:?PROJECT_NUMBER is required}"
STATUS_FIELD="${STATUS_FIELD:-Status}"
TARGET_STATUS="${TARGET_STATUS:-Rejected}"
REQUIRE_LABEL="${REQUIRE_LABEL:-}"
ADD_IF_MISSING="${ADD_IF_MISSING:-false}"

command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "curl not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }
command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "python3 not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }

OWNER="${REPO%%/*}"
NAME="${REPO##*/}"

gql() {
  local resp
  resp="$(curl -sS -X POST "https://api.github.com/graphql" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "$(QUERY="$1" VARS="$2" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"query":os.environ["QUERY"],"variables":json.loads(os.environ["VARS"])}))')")"
  RESP="$resp" python3 -c 'import os,sys,json
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
if d.get("errors"):
    sys.stderr.write("GraphQL error: %s\n" % json.dumps(d["errors"]))
    sys.exit(1)' || { echo "$resp" >&2; exit 1; }
  printf '%s' "$resp"
}

# 1) Resolve project id + Status single-select field id + target option id.
PROJ_Q='query($owner:String!,$number:Int!,$field:String!){organization(login:$owner){projectV2(number:$number){id field(name:$field){... on ProjectV2SingleSelectField{id options{id name}}}}}}'
PROJ_V="$(POWNER="$PROJECT_OWNER" PNUM="$PROJECT_NUMBER" PFIELD="$STATUS_FIELD" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"owner":os.environ["POWNER"],"number":int(os.environ["PNUM"]),"field":os.environ["PFIELD"]}))')"
PROJ_RESP="$(gql "$PROJ_Q" "$PROJ_V")"
eval "$(RESP="$PROJ_RESP" TARGET="$TARGET_STATUS" python3 -c 'import os,json,sys
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
p=((d.get("data") or {}).get("organization") or {}).get("projectV2")
if not p:
    sys.stderr.write("Project not found for owner/number\n"); sys.exit(1)
f=p.get("field") or {}
if not f.get("id"):
    sys.stderr.write("Status field not found (check STATUS_FIELD name)\n"); sys.exit(1)
opt=""
for o in f.get("options",[]):
    if o["name"].strip().lower()==os.environ["TARGET"].strip().lower():
        opt=o["id"]; break
if not opt:
    sys.stderr.write("Target status option not found\n"); sys.exit(1)
print("PROJECT_ID=%s" % json.dumps(p["id"]))
print("FIELD_ID=%s" % json.dumps(f["id"]))
print("OPTION_ID=%s" % json.dumps(opt))')"

# 2) Resolve the PR node id, labels, and existing project item.
PR_Q='query($owner:String!,$name:String!,$pr:Int!){repository(owner:$owner,name:$name){pullRequest(number:$pr){id labels(first:50){nodes{name}} projectItems(first:50){nodes{id project{id number}}}}}}'
PR_V="$(POWNER="$OWNER" PNAME="$NAME" PPR="$PR_NUMBER" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"owner":os.environ["POWNER"],"name":os.environ["PNAME"],"pr":int(os.environ["PPR"])}))')"
PR_RESP="$(gql "$PR_Q" "$PR_V")"
eval "$(RESP="$PR_RESP" PID="$PROJECT_ID" REQ="$REQUIRE_LABEL" python3 -c 'import os,json,sys
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
pr=((d.get("data") or {}).get("repository") or {}).get("pullRequest")
if not pr:
    sys.stderr.write("PR not found\n"); sys.exit(1)
req=os.environ.get("REQ","").strip().lower()
labels=[(n.get("name") or "").strip().lower() for n in (pr.get("labels") or {}).get("nodes",[])]
has="1" if (not req or req in labels) else "0"
item=""
for n in (pr.get("projectItems") or {}).get("nodes",[]):
    if ((n.get("project") or {}).get("id"))==os.environ["PID"]:
        item=n["id"]; break
print("PR_NODE_ID=%s" % json.dumps(pr["id"]))
print("ITEM_ID=%s" % json.dumps(item))
print("HAS_LABEL=%s" % has)')"

# Gate on the required label.
if [ "$HAS_LABEL" != "1" ]; then
  echo "PR #${PR_NUMBER} is missing required label '${REQUIRE_LABEL}'; skipping."
  printf '{"moved":false,"reason":"missing_label","pr_number":"%s"}\n' "$PR_NUMBER" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
  exit 0
fi

# 3) If the PR is not on the board, optionally add it (default: skip).
if [ -z "$ITEM_ID" ]; then
  if [ "$ADD_IF_MISSING" = "true" ]; then
    echo "PR not on board yet; adding it to project ${PROJECT_NUMBER}"
    ADD_Q='mutation($project:ID!,$content:ID!){addProjectV2ItemById(input:{projectId:$project,contentId:$content}){item{id}}}'
    ADD_V="$(PPROJ="$PROJECT_ID" PCONTENT="$PR_NODE_ID" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"project":os.environ["PPROJ"],"content":os.environ["PCONTENT"]}))')"
    ADD_RESP="$(gql "$ADD_Q" "$ADD_V")"
    ITEM_ID="$(RESP="$ADD_RESP" python3 -c 'import os,json;d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"]);print((((d.get("data") or {}).get("addProjectV2ItemById") or {}).get("item") or {}).get("id",""))')"
    if [ -z "$ITEM_ID" ]; then echo "Failed to add PR to project" >&2; exit 1; fi
  else
    echo "PR #${PR_NUMBER} is not on project ${PROJECT_NUMBER}; skipping."
    printf '{"moved":false,"reason":"not_on_board","pr_number":"%s"}\n' "$PR_NUMBER" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
    exit 0
  fi
fi

# 4) Move the card: set the Status single-select field to the target option.
UPD_Q='mutation($project:ID!,$item:ID!,$field:ID!,$opt:String!){updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue(input:{projectId:$project,itemId:$item,fieldId:$field,value:{singleSelectOptionId:$opt}}){projectV2Item{id}}}'
UPD_V="$(PPROJ="$PROJECT_ID" PITEM="$ITEM_ID" PFIELD="$FIELD_ID" POPT="$OPTION_ID" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"project":os.environ["PPROJ"],"item":os.environ["PITEM"],"field":os.environ["PFIELD"],"opt":os.environ["POPT"]}))')"
gql "$UPD_Q" "$UPD_V" >/dev/null

echo "Moved PR #${PR_NUMBER} to '${TARGET_STATUS}'."
printf '{"moved":true,"pr_number":"%s","status":"%s"}\n' "$PR_NUMBER" "$TARGET_STATUS" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"

configuration.content: ✨ Started work on [{{ root().data.issue.title }}](<{{ $["Open Draft PR"].data.html_url }}>).

configuration.text:
## Human Review

On any PR opened with the factory, humans can ask for followups by mentioning @superplanehq in a comment.

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${GITHUB_TOKEN:?GITHUB_TOKEN is required}"
: "${REPO:?REPO is required}"
: "${PR_NUMBER:?PR_NUMBER is required}"
: "${PROJECT_OWNER:?PROJECT_OWNER is required}"
: "${PROJECT_NUMBER:?PROJECT_NUMBER is required}"
STATUS_FIELD="${STATUS_FIELD:-Status}"
TARGET_STATUS="${TARGET_STATUS:-Human Review}"

command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "curl not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }
command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "python3 not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }

OWNER="${REPO%%/*}"
NAME="${REPO##*/}"

# Run a GraphQL request; fail loudly on GraphQL errors.
gql() {
  local resp
  resp="$(curl -sS -X POST "https://api.github.com/graphql" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "$(QUERY="$1" VARS="$2" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"query":os.environ["QUERY"],"variables":json.loads(os.environ["VARS"])}))')")"
  RESP="$resp" python3 -c 'import os,sys,json
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
if d.get("errors"):
    sys.stderr.write("GraphQL error: %s\n" % json.dumps(d["errors"]))
    sys.exit(1)' || { echo "$resp" >&2; exit 1; }
  printf '%s' "$resp"
}

# 1) Resolve project id + Status single-select field id + target option id.
PROJ_Q='query($owner:String!,$number:Int!,$field:String!){organization(login:$owner){projectV2(number:$number){id field(name:$field){... on ProjectV2SingleSelectField{id options{id name}}}}}}'
PROJ_V="$(POWNER="$PROJECT_OWNER" PNUM="$PROJECT_NUMBER" PFIELD="$STATUS_FIELD" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"owner":os.environ["POWNER"],"number":int(os.environ["PNUM"]),"field":os.environ["PFIELD"]}))')"
PROJ_RESP="$(gql "$PROJ_Q" "$PROJ_V")"
eval "$(RESP="$PROJ_RESP" TARGET="$TARGET_STATUS" python3 -c 'import os,json,sys
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
p=((d.get("data") or {}).get("organization") or {}).get("projectV2")
if not p:
    sys.stderr.write("Project not found for owner/number\n"); sys.exit(1)
f=p.get("field") or {}
if not f.get("id"):
    sys.stderr.write("Status field not found (check STATUS_FIELD name)\n"); sys.exit(1)
opt=""
for o in f.get("options",[]):
    if o["name"].strip().lower()==os.environ["TARGET"].strip().lower():
        opt=o["id"]; break
if not opt:
    sys.stderr.write("Target status option not found\n"); sys.exit(1)
print("PROJECT_ID=%s" % json.dumps(p["id"]))
print("FIELD_ID=%s" % json.dumps(f["id"]))
print("OPTION_ID=%s" % json.dumps(opt))')"

# 2) Resolve the PR node id and its existing project item (if already on the board).
PR_Q='query($owner:String!,$name:String!,$pr:Int!){repository(owner:$owner,name:$name){pullRequest(number:$pr){id projectItems(first:50){nodes{id project{id number}}}}}}'
PR_V="$(POWNER="$OWNER" PNAME="$NAME" PPR="$PR_NUMBER" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"owner":os.environ["POWNER"],"name":os.environ["PNAME"],"pr":int(os.environ["PPR"])}))')"
PR_RESP="$(gql "$PR_Q" "$PR_V")"
eval "$(RESP="$PR_RESP" PID="$PROJECT_ID" python3 -c 'import os,json,sys
d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"])
pr=((d.get("data") or {}).get("repository") or {}).get("pullRequest")
if not pr:
    sys.stderr.write("PR not found\n"); sys.exit(1)
item=""
for n in (pr.get("projectItems") or {}).get("nodes",[]):
    if ((n.get("project") or {}).get("id"))==os.environ["PID"]:
        item=n["id"]; break
print("PR_NODE_ID=%s" % json.dumps(pr["id"]))
print("ITEM_ID=%s" % json.dumps(item))')"

# 3) If the PR is not on the board yet, add it.
if [ -z "$ITEM_ID" ]; then
  echo "PR not on board yet; adding it to project ${PROJECT_NUMBER}"
  ADD_Q='mutation($project:ID!,$content:ID!){addProjectV2ItemById(input:{projectId:$project,contentId:$content}){item{id}}}'
  ADD_V="$(PPROJ="$PROJECT_ID" PCONTENT="$PR_NODE_ID" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"project":os.environ["PPROJ"],"content":os.environ["PCONTENT"]}))')"
  ADD_RESP="$(gql "$ADD_Q" "$ADD_V")"
  ITEM_ID="$(RESP="$ADD_RESP" python3 -c 'import os,json;d=json.loads(os.environ["RESP"]);print((((d.get("data") or {}).get("addProjectV2ItemById") or {}).get("item") or {}).get("id",""))')"
  if [ -z "$ITEM_ID" ]; then echo "Failed to add PR to project" >&2; exit 1; fi
fi

# 4) Move the card: set the Status single-select field to the target option.
UPD_Q='mutation($project:ID!,$item:ID!,$field:ID!,$opt:String!){updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue(input:{projectId:$project,itemId:$item,fieldId:$field,value:{singleSelectOptionId:$opt}}){projectV2Item{id}}}'
UPD_V="$(PPROJ="$PROJECT_ID" PITEM="$ITEM_ID" PFIELD="$FIELD_ID" POPT="$OPTION_ID" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"project":os.environ["PPROJ"],"item":os.environ["PITEM"],"field":os.environ["PFIELD"],"opt":os.environ["POPT"]}))')"
gql "$UPD_Q" "$UPD_V" >/dev/null

echo "Moved PR #${PR_NUMBER} to '${TARGET_STATUS}'."
printf '{"moved":true,"pr_number":"%s","status":"%s"}\n' "$PR_NUMBER" "$TARGET_STATUS" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"

configuration.text:
## Creating a working branch

We are creating a branch to have checkpoints, and imidiately opening a PR
as a place where to store session logs

## Ready for review

Mark the PR as ready for review, and wait for either human guidance, or merge.

## Bookkeeping

When a PR is closed, we either mark it a success or a failure and move it to the appropriate column.

## Implementation

Implement the changes and push to the branch

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -uo pipefail

: "${GITHUB_TOKEN:?GITHUB_TOKEN is required}"
: "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:?ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is required}"
: "${REPO:?REPO is required}"
: "${BRANCH:?BRANCH is required}"
ISSUE_NUMBER="${ISSUE_NUMBER:-unknown}"
ISSUE_TITLE="${ISSUE_TITLE:-}"
SEMAPHORE_ORG_URL="${SEMAPHORE_ORG_URL:-}"
PIPELINE_ID="${PIPELINE_ID:-}"
RAW="$(mktemp)"

emit() { BRANCH="$BRANCH" python3 -c 'import os,json;print(json.dumps({"branch":os.environ.get("BRANCH",""),"fixed":True}))' > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"; }

for bin in git curl python3; do
  command -v "$bin" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "$bin not found on runner host" >&2; emit; exit 0; }
done

# Claude is not installed on the host: install the Claude Code CLI (user-level, no root needed).
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.claude/bin:$PATH"
command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "Claude Code install failed" >&2; emit; exit 0; }

git config --global user.email "automation@superplane.dev"
git config --global user.name "SuperPlane Automation"

WORKDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
git clone --branch "$BRANCH" "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${REPO}.git" "$WORKDIR" || { echo "git clone failed" >&2; emit; exit 0; }
cd "$WORKDIR"

# DCO: force a Signed-off-by trailer on EVERY commit in this repo, including Claude Code's own internal commits.
mkdir -p .git/hooks
cat > .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg <<'HOOK'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
MSG_FILE="$1"
SOB="Signed-off-by: SuperPlane Automation <automation@superplane.dev>"
grep -qF "$SOB" "$MSG_FILE" || printf '\n%s\n' "$SOB" >> "$MSG_FILE"
HOOK
chmod +x .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg

cat > /tmp/sp_format.py <<'PYEOF'
import sys, json
for line in sys.stdin:
    line = line.strip()
    if not line:
        continue
    try:
        e = json.loads(line)
    except Exception:
        print(line, flush=True)
        continue
    t = e.get("type")
    if t == "system":
        print("=== session started (model=%s) ===" % e.get("model", "?"), flush=True)
    elif t == "assistant":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            bt = b.get("type")
            if bt == "text":
                txt = (b.get("text") or "").strip()
                if txt:
                    print("\n" + txt, flush=True)
            elif bt == "tool_use":
                inp = b.get("input", {}) or {}
                detail = inp.get("command") or inp.get("file_path") or inp.get("path") or json.dumps(inp)[:200]
                print("  -> %s: %s" % (b.get("name"), detail), flush=True)
    elif t == "user":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            if b.get("type") == "tool_result":
                c = b.get("content", "")
                if isinstance(c, list):
                    c = " ".join(x.get("text", "") for x in c if isinstance(x, dict))
                c = str(c).strip().replace("\n", " ")
                if c:
                    print("     <- %s" % c[:300], flush=True)
    elif t == "result":
        ok = "ok" if not e.get("is_error") else "ERROR"
        print("\n=== done [%s] turns=%s cost=$%s ===" % (ok, e.get("num_turns"), e.get("total_cost_usd")), flush=True)
PYEOF

read -r -d '' PROMPT <<EOF || true
CI is failing on this branch for a fix to GitHub issue #${ISSUE_NUMBER} (${ISSUE_TITLE}).

Repository: ${REPO}
Branch: ${BRANCH}
Semaphore pipeline id: ${PIPELINE_ID}
Semaphore API base: ${SEMAPHORE_ORG_URL}/api/v1alpha (authenticate with the token in the SEMAPHORE_API_TOKEN environment variable, header "Authorization: Token \$SEMAPHORE_API_TOKEN")

1/ Investigate the CI failure. Fetch the failing pipeline (${PIPELINE_ID}) and its job logs from the Semaphore API, or reproduce the failure locally by running the project's tests/build.
2/ Fix the root cause. Keep the change focused and clean; prefer long-term fixes over quick patches. Add or update tests where it makes sense.
3/ Commit your changes to the current branch with a clear message.

Output only a JSON result in this format: {"summary": ["short bullet 1", "short bullet 2"]}
EOF

claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose --output-format stream-json 2>&1 \
  | tee "$RAW" | python3 -u /tmp/sp_format.py || true

git add -A
git commit -s -m "fix: address CI failures for #${ISSUE_NUMBER}" || echo "Nothing new to commit"
git push origin "$BRANCH" || echo "git push failed" >&2

echo "Pushed a fix attempt to ${BRANCH}."
emit
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${GITHUB_TOKEN:?GITHUB_TOKEN is required}"
: "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:?ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is required}"
: "${REPO:?REPO is required}"
: "${PR_NUMBER:?PR_NUMBER is required}"
COMMENT_BODY="${COMMENT_BODY:-}"
RAW="$(mktemp)"

for bin in git curl python3; do
  command -v "$bin" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "$bin not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }
done

# The issue_comment payload has no head branch, so resolve the PR head branch from the API.
BRANCH="$(curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  "https://api.github.com/repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}" \
  | python3 -c 'import sys,json
try:
    print((json.load(sys.stdin).get("head") or {}).get("ref",""))
except Exception:
    print("")')"
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
  echo "Could not resolve head branch for ${REPO}#${PR_NUMBER}" >&2
  exit 1
fi
echo "Resolved PR #${PR_NUMBER} head branch: ${BRANCH}"

# Claude is not installed on the host: install the Claude Code CLI (user-level, no root needed).
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.claude/bin:$PATH"
command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "Claude Code install failed" >&2; exit 1; }

git config --global user.email "automation@superplane.dev"
git config --global user.name "SuperPlane Automation"

WORKDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
git clone --depth 1 --branch "$BRANCH" \
  "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${REPO}.git" "$WORKDIR"
cd "$WORKDIR"

# DCO: force a Signed-off-by trailer on EVERY commit in this repo, including Claude Code's own internal commits.
mkdir -p .git/hooks
cat > .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg <<'HOOK'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
MSG_FILE="$1"
SOB="Signed-off-by: SuperPlane Automation <automation@superplane.dev>"
grep -qF "$SOB" "$MSG_FILE" || printf '\n%s\n' "$SOB" >> "$MSG_FILE"
HOOK
chmod +x .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg

read -r -d '' PROMPT <<EOF || true
You are addressing a reviewer's requested changes on an open pull request.

Repository: ${REPO}
Pull Request Number: ${PR_NUMBER}

The comment that triggered this run:
---
${COMMENT_BODY}
---

1/ Gather context. Using the provided GitHub token, read the PR (#${PR_NUMBER}), its conversation, and all review comments before writing any code.
2/ Implement the requested changes described in the comment above.
3/ Keep the change focused and commit your work to the current branch with clear commit messages.

Guidelines:

1/ Focus on clean code.
2/ Make long term fixes, not fast patches
3/ Add tests where it makes sense.

The output should contain a bullet point summary of changes in the following format:

{
   "summary": [
      "example bullet point 1",
      "example bullet point 2",
   }
}

Output only the JSON result. Keep the bulletpoints short, concise and to the point.
EOF

# Human-readable formatter for Claude Code stream-json events.
cat > /tmp/sp_format.py <<'PYEOF'
import sys, json
for line in sys.stdin:
    line = line.strip()
    if not line:
        continue
    try:
        e = json.loads(line)
    except Exception:
        print(line, flush=True)
        continue
    t = e.get("type")
    if t == "system":
        print("=== session started (model=%s) ===" % e.get("model", "?"), flush=True)
    elif t == "assistant":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            bt = b.get("type")
            if bt == "text":
                txt = (b.get("text") or "").strip()
                if txt:
                    print("\n" + txt, flush=True)
            elif bt == "tool_use":
                inp = b.get("input", {}) or {}
                detail = inp.get("command") or inp.get("file_path") or inp.get("path") or json.dumps(inp)[:200]
                print("  -> %s: %s" % (b.get("name"), detail), flush=True)
    elif t == "user":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            if b.get("type") == "tool_result":
                c = b.get("content", "")
                if isinstance(c, list):
                    c = " ".join(x.get("text", "") for x in c if isinstance(x, dict))
                c = str(c).strip().replace("\n", " ")
                if c:
                    print("     <- %s" % c[:300], flush=True)
    elif t == "result":
        ok = "ok" if not e.get("is_error") else "ERROR"
        print("\n=== done [%s] turns=%s cost=$%s ===" % (ok, e.get("num_turns"), e.get("total_cost_usd")), flush=True)
PYEOF

# Extractor for the final result JSON from the stream.
cat > /tmp/sp_extract.py <<'PYEOF'
import json, os
summary = ""
try:
    with open(os.environ["RAW"]) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                e = json.loads(line)
            except Exception:
                continue
            if e.get("type") == "result":
                summary = e.get("result", "")
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass
print(json.dumps({"branch": os.environ.get("BRANCH", ""), "summary": summary}))
PYEOF

# Run Claude Code with live, human-readable streaming; keep raw JSONL for result extraction.
if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose --output-format stream-json 2>&1 \
    | tee "$RAW" \
    | python3 -u /tmp/sp_format.py || true
else
  echo "python3 not found; streaming raw JSON" >&2
  claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose --output-format stream-json 2>&1 \
    | tee "$RAW" || true
fi

git add -A
git commit -s -m "chore: apply requested changes on PR #${PR_NUMBER}" || echo "Nothing new to commit"
git push origin "$BRANCH"
echo "Pushed changes to ${BRANCH}"

# Emit node output: branch plus the final summary from Claude.
if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  RAW="$RAW" BRANCH="$BRANCH" python3 /tmp/sp_extract.py > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
else
  printf '{"branch":"%s"}\n' "$BRANCH" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BASE="${BASE:-main}"
SESSION_ID=$(date +%s)
BRANCH="sf-runner/${SESSION_ID}"
GITURL="https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${REPO}.git"
WORKDIR="superplane"

git config --global user.email "automation@superplane.dev"
git config --global user.name "SuperPlane Automation"

git clone --depth 1 "$GITURL" "$WORKDIR"
cd "$WORKDIR"

git checkout -b "$BRANCH"
mkdir -p .superplane/sessions
echo "Starting a new session" > .superplane/sessions/$SESSION_ID.md

git add .superplane
git commit -s -m "fix: Initialize session"
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"

echo "Pushed branch ${BRANCH}"

# Respond with branch and session id
printf '{"branch":"%s", "session":"%s"}\n' "$BRANCH" "$SESSION_ID" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"

nodes.name: Notify failed to get Green CI

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${GITHUB_TOKEN:?GITHUB_TOKEN is required}"
: "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:?ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is required}"
: "${REPO:?REPO is required}"
: "${BRANCH:?BRANCH is required}"

ISSUE_NUMBER="${ISSUE_NUMBER:-unknown}"
ISSUE_TITLE="${ISSUE_TITLE:-}"
SESSION_ID="${SESSION_ID:-}"
RAW="$(mktemp)"

command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "git not found on runner host" >&2; exit 1; }

# Claude is not installed on the host: install the Claude Code CLI (user-level, no root needed).
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.claude/bin:$PATH"
command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "Claude Code install failed" >&2; exit 1; }

git config --global user.email "automation@superplane.dev"
git config --global user.name "SuperPlane Automation"

WORKDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
git clone --depth 1 --branch "$BRANCH" \
  "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${REPO}.git" "$WORKDIR"
cd "$WORKDIR"

# DCO: force a Signed-off-by trailer on EVERY commit in this repo, including Claude Code's own internal commits.
mkdir -p .git/hooks
cat > .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg <<'HOOK'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
MSG_FILE="$1"
SOB="Signed-off-by: SuperPlane Automation <automation@superplane.dev>"
grep -qF "$SOB" "$MSG_FILE" || printf '\n%s\n' "$SOB" >> "$MSG_FILE"
HOOK
chmod +x .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg

read -r -d '' PROMPT <<EOF || true
You are writting an implementation for a GitHub issue on the current branch.

Repository: ${REPO}
Issue number: ${ISSUE_NUMBER}
Issue title: ${ISSUE_TITLE}

1/ Gather context. Using the provided GitHub token, read the FULL issue and ALL of its comments before writing any code.
2/ Write a plane into .superplane/sessions/${SESSION_ID}.md. The plan is required to resolve the issue and address the discussion in the comments.
3/ Keep the plan focused and commit your work to the current branch with clear commit messages.
4/ Focus on long term success, rather than short term fixes, unless a human told you otherwise in the issue.

Guidelines:

1/ Write clearly, and for humans.
2/ Keep it short, to the point, and concise.
3/ Format it nicely.
4/ Add architectural mermaid diagrams if the problem requires it.
5/ Describe pros/cons of the approach that you want to make.
6/ Describe tradeoffs.

EOF

# Human-readable formatter for Claude Code stream-json events.
cat > /tmp/sp_format.py <<'PYEOF'
import sys, json
for line in sys.stdin:
    line = line.strip()
    if not line:
        continue
    try:
        e = json.loads(line)
    except Exception:
        print(line, flush=True)
        continue
    t = e.get("type")
    if t == "system":
        print("=== session started (model=%s) ===" % e.get("model", "?"), flush=True)
    elif t == "assistant":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            bt = b.get("type")
            if bt == "text":
                txt = (b.get("text") or "").strip()
                if txt:
                    print("\n" + txt, flush=True)
            elif bt == "tool_use":
                inp = b.get("input", {}) or {}
                detail = inp.get("command") or inp.get("file_path") or inp.get("path") or json.dumps(inp)[:200]
                print("  -> %s: %s" % (b.get("name"), detail), flush=True)
    elif t == "user":
        for b in e.get("message", {}).get("content", []):
            if b.get("type") == "tool_result":
                c = b.get("content", "")
                if isinstance(c, list):
                    c = " ".join(x.get("text", "") for x in c if isinstance(x, dict))
                c = str(c).strip().replace("\n", " ")
                if c:
                    print("     <- %s" % c[:300], flush=True)
    elif t == "result":
        ok = "ok" if not e.get("is_error") else "ERROR"
        print("\n=== done [%s] turns=%s cost=$%s ===" % (ok, e.get("num_turns"), e.get("total_cost_usd")), flush=True)
PYEOF

# Extractor for the final result JSON from the stream.
cat > /tmp/sp_extract.py <<'PYEOF'
import json, os
summary = ""
try:
    with open(os.environ["RAW"]) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                e = json.loads(line)
            except Exception:
                continue
            if e.get("type") == "result":
                summary = e.get("result", "")
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass
print(json.dumps({"branch": os.environ.get("BRANCH", ""), "summary": summary}))
PYEOF

# Run Claude Code with live, human-readable streaming; keep raw JSONL for result extraction.
if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose --output-format stream-json 2>&1 \
    | tee "$RAW" \
    | python3 -u /tmp/sp_format.py || true
else
  echo "python3 not found; streaming raw JSON" >&2
  claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose --output-format stream-json 2>&1 \
    | tee "$RAW" || true
fi

git add -A
git commit -s -m "fix: implement issue #${ISSUE_NUMBER} via Claude Code" || echo "Nothing new to commit"
git push origin "$BRANCH"
echo "Pushed implementation to ${BRANCH}"

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BASE="${BASE:-main}"
SESSION_ID=$(date +%s)
BRANCH="sf-runner/${SESSION_ID}"
GITURL="https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${REPO}.git"
WORKDIR="superplane"

git config --global user.email "automation@superplane.dev"
git config --global user.name "SuperPlane Automation"

git clone --depth 1 "$GITURL" "$WORKDIR"
cd "$WORKDIR"

git checkout -b "$BRANCH"
mkdir -p .superplane/sessions
echo "Starting a new session" > .superplane/sessions/$SESSION_ID.md

git add .superplane
git commit -s -m "fix: Initialize session"
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"

echo "Pushed branch ${BRANCH}"

# Respond with branch and session id
printf '{"branch":"%s", "session":"%s"}\n' "$BRANCH" "$SESSION_ID" > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"

Taking a look!

result.summary:
The implementation is complete and committed. All backend tests (models, action, producer-guard), the frontend spec, TypeScript type-check, ESLint, revive, gofmt, prettier, and the gRPC-actions-status and DB-structure checks pass.

{
   "summary": [
      "Added an atomic CancelRun RPC (POST /api/v1/canvases/{canvas_id}/runs/{run_id}/cancel) replacing the racy client-side DescribeRun + N CancelExecution + M DeleteNodeQueueItem fan-out",
      "Added models.CancelRunInTransaction: locks the run FOR UPDATE, cancels active executions, deletes queued items, completes pending requests, and finalizes the run as result=cancelled",
      "Added cancelled_at/cancelled_by columns to workflow_runs (migration + model + serializer + proto) as the cancellation marker and audit trail",
      "Made run finalization honor the cancelled marker so a cancelled run always resolves to result=cancelled",
      "Extracted the component external Cancel hook from cancel_execution and run it best-effort after commit (outside the run lock)",
      "Added producer guards in NodeQueueWorker and EventRouter to skip creating new work for a finished/cancelled run",
      "Wired authorization rule (canvases:update) and regenerated protobuf/OpenAPI/API clients",
      "Collapsed the frontend Stop action to a single canvasesCancelRun call, enabled while the run is STARTED",
      "Added model, action, and producer-guard tests plus updated the frontend hook spec"
   ]
}
